map_width = 270px | coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|3.5|N|135|51|52.3|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | county = | region = [[Nara Park]], [[Nara (city)|Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | country = Japan | elevation = | area = {{convert|298.6|ha}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/119909 |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara - Map of the inscribed property Clarification / adopted |date=2012 |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} | max_area = | date_max_area = | status = | established = | visitation = | visitation_year = | events = | authority = | website = | ecosystem = | classification_WWF = | classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] [[Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=draft|12=draft}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=draft|12=draft}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:5]] [[Category:11 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11 5]] [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] [[Category:Old-growth forests]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]]

caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=draft|12=draft}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11 5]] [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] [[Category:Old-growth forests]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]]

protected as a [[Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] [[Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=draft|12=draft}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | (left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 5]] [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] [[Category:Old-growth forests]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]]

map_width = 270px | coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|3.5|N|135|51|52.3|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | county = | region = [[Nara Park]], [[Nara (city)|Nara]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | country = Japan | elevation = | area = {{convert|298.6|ha}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/119909 |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara - Map of the inscribed property Clarification / adopted |date=2012 |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} | max_area = | date_max_area = | status = | established = | visitation = | visitation_year = | events = | authority = | website = | ecosystem = | classification_WWF = | classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] [[Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=draft|12=draft}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=draft|12=draft}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] [[Category:Old-growth forests]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]]

(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] [[Category:Old-growth forests]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]]

{{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] [[Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | (antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] [[Category:Old-growth forests]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]]

Japan | elevation = | area = {{convert|298.6|ha}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/119909 |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara - Map of the inscribed property Clarification / adopted |date=2012 |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} | max_area = | date_max_area = | status = | established = | visitation = | visitation_year = | events = | authority = | website = | ecosystem = | classification_WWF = | classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] [[Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|qq=}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 5]] [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] [[Category:Old-growth forests]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|qq=}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} [[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]] [[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]] [[ja:春日山原始林]] [[ko:가스가산 원시림]] [[simple:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[zh:春日山原始林]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|qq=}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} [[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]] [[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]] [[ja:春日山原始林]] [[ko:가스가산 원시림]] [[simple:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[zh:春日山原始林]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} [[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]] [[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]] [[ja:春日山原始林]] [[ko:가스가산 원시림]] [[simple:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[zh:春日山原始林]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

|url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|qq=}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[:en:List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|List of Natural Monuments of = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[:en:Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[UNESCO]] [[World Heritage Site]] [[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|qq=}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[:en:List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} [[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]] [[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]] [[ja:春日山原始林]] [[ko:가스가산 원시림]] [[simple:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[zh:春日山原始林]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Primeval forest in Nara, Japan}} {{Infobox forest | name = Kasugayama Primeval Forest | native_name = 春日山原始林 | native_lang = ja | native_name2 = | native_lang2 = | photo = Kasugayama Primeval Forest 02.jpg | photo_caption = | photo_width = | map = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Primeval Forest | map_width = 270px | coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|3.5|N|135|51|52.3|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | county = | region = [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | country = Japan | elevation = | area = {{convert|298.6|ha}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/119909 |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara - Map of the inscribed property Clarification / adopted |date=2012 |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} | max_area = | date_max_area = | status = | established = | visitation = | visitation_year = | events = | authority = | website = | ecosystem = | classification_WWF = | classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[:en:Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] [[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|qq=}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:Place of Scenic Beauty|Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[:en:List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:Protected Forests (Japan)|Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} [[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]] [[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]] [[ja:春日山原始林]] [[ko:가스가산 원시림]] [[simple:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[zh:春日山原始林]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:Place of Scenic Beauty|Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[:en:List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:Protected Forests (Japan)|Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[:en:Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] [[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|qq=}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:Place of Scenic Beauty|Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q5367406]] Wikidata name: Kasugayama Primeval Forest [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q5367406]] Wikidata name: Kasugayama Primeval Forest [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Primeval forest in Nara, Japan}} {{Infobox forest | name = Kasugayama Primeval Forest | native_name = 春日山原始林 | native_lang = ja | native_name2 = | native_lang2 = | photo = Kasugayama Primeval Forest 02.jpg | photo_caption = | photo_width = | map = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Primeval Forest | map_width = 270px | coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|3.5|N|135|51|52.3|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | county = | region = [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | country = Japan | elevation = | area = {{convert|298.6|ha}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/119909 |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara - Map of the inscribed property Clarification / adopted |date=2012 |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} | max_area = | date_max_area = | status = | established = | visitation = | visitation_year = | events = | authority = | website = | ecosystem = | classification_WWF = | classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[:en:Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] [[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|qq=|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:Place of Scenic Beauty|Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[:en:List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:Protected Forests (Japan)|Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] }} [[sn:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} [[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]] [[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]] [[ja:春日山原始林]] [[ko:가스가산 원시림]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[zh:春日山原始林]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''

Park|Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|qq=|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:Place of Scenic Beauty|Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[:en:List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:Protected Forests (Japan)|Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== (second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:Place of Scenic Beauty|Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the [[sn:commons:Category:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]][[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]][[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]][[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]][[ja:春日山原始林]][[ko:가스가산 원시림]][[simple:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]][[tr:Kasugayama Bakir Ormanı]][[zh:春日山原始林]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q5367406]] Wikidata name: Kasugayama Primeval Forest [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q5367406]] Wikidata name: Kasugayama Primeval Forest [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Primeval forest in Nara, Japan}} {{Infobox forest | name = Kasugayama Primeval Forest | native_name = 春日山原始林 | native_lang = ja | native_name2 = | native_lang2 = | photo = Kasugayama Primeval Forest 02.jpg | photo_caption = | photo_width = | map = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Primeval Forest | map_width = 270px | coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|3.5|N|135|51|52.3|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | county = | region = [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | country = Japan | elevation = | area = {{convert|298.6|ha}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/119909 |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara - Map of the inscribed property Clarification / adopted |date=2012 |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} | max_area = | date_max_area = | status = | established = | visitation = | visitation_year = | events = | authority = | website = | ecosystem = | classification_WWF = | classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[:en:Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] [[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:Place of Scenic Beauty|Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[:en:List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:Protected Forests (Japan)|Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] }} [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} [[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]] [[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]] [[ja:春日山原始林]] [[ko:가스가산 원시림]] [[Category:Pages
