Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Primeval Forest | map_width = 270px | coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|3.5|N|135|51|52.3|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | county = | region = [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | country = Japan | elevation = | area = {{convert|298.6|ha}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/119909 |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara - Map of the inscribed property Clarification / adopted |date=2012 |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} | max_area = | date_max_area = | status = | established = | visitation = | visitation_year = | events = | authority = | website = | ecosystem = | classification_WWF = | classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[:en:Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] [[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:Place of Scenic Beauty|Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[:en:List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:Protected Forests (Japan)|Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:Place of Scenic Beauty|Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[:en:List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:Protected Forests (Japan)|Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[:en:Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] [[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:Place of Scenic Beauty|Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[:en:List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:Protected Forests (Japan)|Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Nara, Nara]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]] [[Category:Forests of Japan]] }} [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} [[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]] [[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]] [[ja:春日山原始林]] [[ko:가스가산 원시림]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] [[zh:春日山原始林]] {{wikidata link|Q5367406}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

native_name = 春日山原始林 | native_lang = ja | native_name2 = | native_lang2 = | photo = Kasugayama Primeval Forest 02.jpg | photo_caption = | photo_width = | map = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Primeval Forest | map_width = 270px | coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|3.5|N|135|51|52.3|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | county = | region = [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]], [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | country = Japan | elevation = | area = {{convert|298.6|ha}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/119909 |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara - Map of the inscribed property Clarification / adopted |date=2012 |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of [[:en:Old-growth forest|primeval forest]] in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], Japan, that is protected as a [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]] and which forms part of the [[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] [[:en:World Heritage Site|World Heritage Site]] [[:en:Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara]].{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher=[[:en:UNESCO|UNESCO]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Jōwa]] 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ninmyō|Emperor Ninmyō]], a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 [[:en:Nara Park|Nara Park]] opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and [[:en:Mount Wakakusa|Mount Wakakusa]]; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a [[:en:Typhoon|Typhoon]].{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a [[:en:Place of Scenic Beauty|Place of Scenic Beauty]] under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of [[:en:Special Natural Monument|Special Natural Monument]], under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * [[:en:List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan]] * [[:en:List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments]] * [[:en:List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] * [[:en:Protected Forests (Japan)|Protected Forests (Japan)]] ==References== Basoa]][[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]][[ja:春日山原始林]][[ko:가스가산 원시림]][[simple:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]][[tr:Kasugayama Bakir Ormanı]][[zh:春日山原始林]] [[co:commons:Category:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]][[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]][[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]][[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]][[ja:春日山原始林]][[ko:가스가산 원시림]][[simple:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]][[tr:Kasugayama Bakir Ormanı]][[zh:春日山原始林]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]][[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]][[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]][[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]][[ja:春日山原始林]][[ko:가스가산 원시림]][[simple:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]][[tr:Kasugayama Bakir Ormanı]][[zh:春日山原始林]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q5367406]] Wikidata name: Kasugayama Primeval Forest [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q5367406]] Wikidata name: Kasugayama Primeval Forest [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Primeval forest in Nara, Japan}} {{Infobox forest | name = Kasugayama Primeval Forest | native_name = 春日山原始林 | native_lang = ja | native_name2 = | native_lang2 = | photo = Kasugayama Primeval Forest 02.jpg | photo_caption = | photo_width = | map = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Primeval Forest | map_width = 270px | coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|3.5|N|135|51|52.3|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | county = | region = {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} | country = Japan | elevation = | area = {{convert|298.6|ha}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/119909 |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara - Map of the inscribed property Clarification / adopted |date=2012 |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} |access-date=25 October 2023}} | max_area = | date_max_area = | status = | established = | visitation = | visitation_year = | events = | authority = | website = | ecosystem = | classification_WWF = | classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of {{ill|Old-growth forest|en|Old-growth forest|WD=Q208478|lt=primeval forest}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, Japan, that is protected as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument}} and which forms part of the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|WD=Q1055749|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}} to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ninmyō|en|Emperor Ninmyō|WD=Q349271|lt=Emperor Ninmyō}}, a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}} opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|WD=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa}}; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a {{ill|Typhoon|en|Typhoon|WD=Q140588|lt=Typhoon}}.{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a {{ill|Place of Scenic Beauty|en|Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty}} under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument}}, under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * {{ill|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|en|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|WD=Q1156968|lt=List of World Heritage Sites in Japan}} * {{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|WD=Q20982902|lt=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}} * {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q25094789|lt=List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)}} * {{ill|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q6594203|lt=List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}} * {{ill|Protected Forests (Japan)|en|Protected Forests (Japan)|WD=Q106611640|lt=Protected Forests (Japan)}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] {{Commons category|Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} [[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]] [[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]] events = | authority = | website = | ecosystem = | classification_WWF = | classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of {{ill|Old-growth forest|en|Old-growth forest|WD=Q208478|lt=primeval forest}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, Japan, that is protected as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument}} and which forms part of the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|WD=Q1055749|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}} to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ninmyō|en|Emperor Ninmyō|WD=Q349271|lt=Emperor Ninmyō}}, a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}} opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|WD=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa}}; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a {{ill|Typhoon|en|Typhoon|WD=Q140588|lt=Typhoon}}.{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a {{ill|Place of Scenic Beauty|en|Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty}} under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument}}, under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== {{Commons Category:11]] [[Category}} * {{ill|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|en|List of World Beauty|WD=Q11414752|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty}} under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument}}, under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== [[Category:Category]] * {{ill|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|en|List of World Heritage Sites in = {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} | country = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of {{ill|Old-growth forest|en|Old-growth forest|WD=Q208478|lt=primeval forest}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, Japan, that is protected as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument}} and which forms part of the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|WD=Q1055749|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}} to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ninmyō|en|Emperor Ninmyō|WD=Q349271|lt=Emperor Ninmyō}}, a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}} opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|WD=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa}}; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a {{ill|Typhoon|en|Typhoon|WD=Q140588|lt=Typhoon}}.{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a {{ill|Place of Scenic Beauty|en|Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty}} under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument}}, under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kasugayama Primeval Forest | map_width = 270px | coordinates = {{Coord|34|41|3.5|N|135|51|52.3|E|region:JP-29|display=inline, title}} | county = | region = {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} | country = Japan | elevation = | area = {{convert|298.6|ha}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/document/119909 |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara - Map of the inscribed property Clarification / adopted |date=2012 |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} |access-date=25 October 2023}} | max_area = | date_max_area = | status = | established = | visitation = | visitation_year = | events = | authority = | website = | ecosystem = | classification_WWF = | classification_EPA = | classification_CEC = | disturbance = | forest_cover = | species = | indicator_plants = | lesser_flora = | fauna = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of {{ill|Old-growth forest|en|Old-growth forest|WD=Q208478|lt=primeval forest}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, Japan, that is protected as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument}} and which forms part of the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|WD=Q1055749|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO}} |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}} to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ninmyō|en|Emperor Ninmyō|WD=Q349271|lt=Emperor Ninmyō}}, a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park}} opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|WD=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa}}; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a {{ill|Typhoon|en|Typhoon|WD=Q140588|lt=Typhoon}}.{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a {{ill|Place of Scenic Beauty|en|Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty}} under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument}}, under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== [[Category:Category]] * {{ill|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|en|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|WD=Q1156968|lt=List of World Heritage Sites in Japan}} * {{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|WD=Q20982902|lt=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}} * {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q25094789|lt=List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)}} * {{ill|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q6594203|lt=List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}} * {{ill|Protected Forests (Japan)|en|Protected Forests (Japan)|WD=Q106611640|lt=Protected Forests (Japan)}} ==References== {{Reflist}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|WD=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa}}; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a {{ill|Typhoon|en|Typhoon|WD=Q140588|lt=Typhoon}}.{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a {{ill|Place of Scenic Beauty|en|Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty}} under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument}}, under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== * {{ill|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|en|List of World Heritage Sites in }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of {{ill|Old-growth forest|en|Old-growth forest|WD=Q208478|lt=primeval forest|1=Old-growth forest}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, Japan, that is protected as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument|1=Special Natural Monument}} and which forms part of the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|WD=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|WD=Q1055749|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|WD=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park|1=Nara Park}} to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ninmyō|en|Emperor Ninmyō|WD=Q349271|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|1=Emperor Ninmyō}}, a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park|1=Nara Park}} opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|WD=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa|1=Mount Wakakusa}}; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a {{ill|Typhoon|en|Typhoon|WD=Q140588|lt=Typhoon|1=Typhoon}}.{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a {{ill|Place of Scenic Beauty|en|Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}} under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument|1=Special Natural Monument}}, under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ninmyō|en|Emperor Ninmyō|WD=Q349271|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|1=Emperor Ninmyō}}, a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|WD=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park|1=Nara Park}} opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|WD=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa|1=Mount Wakakusa}}; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a {{ill|Typhoon|en|Typhoon|WD=Q140588|lt=Typhoon|1=Typhoon}}.{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a {{ill|Place of Scenic Beauty|en|Place of Scenic Beauty|WD=Q11414752|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}} under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|WD=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument|1=Special Natural Monument}}, under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See = }} {{nihongo|'''Kasugayama Primeval Forest'''|春日山原始林|Kasugaya-yama genshi-rin}} is an area of {{convert|298.6|ha}} of {{ill|Old-growth forest|en|Old-growth forest|qid=Q208478|lt=primeval forest|1=Old-growth forest}} in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, Japan, that is protected as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|qid=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument|1=Special Natural Monument}} and which forms part of the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|qid=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|qid=Q1055749|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|qid=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park|1=Nara Park}} to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ninmyō|en|Emperor Ninmyō|qid=Q349271|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|1=Emperor Ninmyō}}, a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|qid=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park|1=Nara Park}} opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|qid=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa|1=Mount Wakakusa}}; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a {{ill|Typhoon|en|Typhoon|qid=Q140588|lt=Typhoon|1=Typhoon}}.{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a {{ill|Place of Scenic Beauty|en|Place of Scenic Beauty|qid=Q11414752|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}} under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|qid=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument|1=Special Natural Monument}}, under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== * {{ill|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|en|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|qid=Q1156968|lt=List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|1=List of World Heritage Sites in Japan}} * {{ill|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|en|List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|qid=Q20982902|lt=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments|1=List of Special Places of Scenic Beauty, Special Historic Sites and Special Natural Monuments}} * {{ill|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q25094789|lt=List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Natural Monuments of Japan (Nara)}} * {{ill|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q6594203|lt=List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Places of Scenic Beauty of Japan (Nara)}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}} * {{ill|Protected Forests (Japan)|en|Protected Forests (Japan)|qid=Q106611640|lt=Protected Forests (Japan)|1=Protected Forests (Japan)}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Kasuga Faith}} [[en:Kasugayama Primeval Forest]] {{Commons category|Kasugayama Primeval Forest}} [[eu:Kasugayamako Jatorrizko Basoa]] [[fr:Forêt primitive de Kasugayama]] Monument|qid=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument|1=Special Natural Monument}} and which forms part of the {{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|qid=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}} {{ill|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|en|Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|qid=Q1055749|lt=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara|1=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.nara.jp/49401.htm |script-title=ja:春日山原始林保全計画 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest Conservation Plan |language=ja |publisher={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} |date=March 2017 |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1951 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}}{{cite web |url=https://whc.unesco.org/en/list/870/ |title=Historic Monuments of Ancient Nara |publisher={{ill|UNESCO|en|UNESCO|qid=Q7809|lt=UNESCO|1=UNESCO}} |access-date=25 October 2023}} Located in {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|qid=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park|1=Nara Park}} to the east of the grounds of [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Taisha]] and a [[Chinju no Mori]],{{cite web |url=https://www.gov-online.go.jp/eng/publicity/book/hlj/html/202107/202107_03_en.html |title=The Forest in the Sacred Precincts of Kasugataisha Shrine |publisher=[[Cabinet Office (Japan)|Cabinet Office]] |date=July 2021 |access-date=25 October 2023}} hunting and logging on {{ill|Mount Kasuga|ja|春日山 (奈良県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kasuga|1=Mount Kasuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513602}} have been prohibited since 841.{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/component/50 |script-title=ja:春日山原始林 |trans-title=Kasugayama Primeval Forest |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} ==History== {{multiple image | align = left | direction = vertical | width1 = 170 | image1 = 春日山原始林（続日本後紀）.jpg | caption1 = The prohibition, in the tenth volume of ''[[Shoku Nihon Kōki]]''
(antepenultimate and penultimate lines, left page) | width2 = 170 | image2 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）1.jpg | caption2 = The background to the prohibition, in the first volume of ''{{ill|Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|ja|類聚三代格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruijū Sandai Kyaku|qq=}}''
(left-hand page) | width3 = 170 | image3 = 春日山原始林（類聚三代格）2.jpg | caption3 = The date, in the continuation of the same: the first day of the third month of {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 8 (841)
(second line, right-hand page) }} In 841, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ninmyō|en|Emperor Ninmyō|qid=Q349271|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|1=Emperor Ninmyō}}, a prohibition was placed on hunting and logging on the sacred mountain.{{rp|7}} In 1873, the [[Daijō-kan|Dajō-kan]] issued a proclamation on the establishment of public parks, and in 1880 {{ill|Nara Park|en|Nara Park|qid=Q1186358|lt=Nara Park|1=Nara Park}} opened.{{rp|10}} This was extended in 1888 to include the areas of Mount Kasuga and {{ill|Mount Wakakusa|en|Mount Wakakusa|qid=Q2428328|lt=Mount Wakakusa|1=Mount Wakakusa}}; that same year, some two thousand trees on Mount Kasuga were felled by a {{ill|Typhoon|en|Typhoon|qid=Q140588|lt=Typhoon|1=Typhoon}}.{{rp|10}} In 1922, Nara Park was [[Cultural Property (Japan)|designated]] a {{ill|Place of Scenic Beauty|en|Place of Scenic Beauty|qid=Q11414752|lt=Place of Scenic Beauty|1=Place of Scenic Beauty}} under the 1919 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#1919 Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law|Historical Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty, and Natural Monuments Preservation Law]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1940 |script-title=ja:奈良公園 |trans-title=Nara Park |language=ja |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |access-date=25 October 2023}} Two years later, Kasugayama Primeval Forest was designated a [[:en:Natural Monument (Japan)|Natural Monument]], a designation upgraded in 1955 to that of {{ill|Special Natural Monument|en|Special Natural Monument|qid=Q96207459|lt=Special Natural Monument|1=Special Natural Monument}}, under the 1950 [[Cultural Property (Japan)#Present 1950 Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties|Law for the Protection of Cultural Properties]]. ==See also== * {{ill|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|en|List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|qid=Q1156968|lt=List of World Heritage Sites in Japan|1=List of
